findings.description: The PDF uses a three-column layout for agenda items (time, topic, presenter) aligned across the page, while the HTML renders these as inline text in a single-column flow.
findings.evidence: PDF shows time slots left-aligned, topics centered, and presenters right-aligned in a tabular-like layout. HTML presents all three elements concatenated on a single line (e.g., '10:00-10:10 Review Agenda and Brief Introductions (Neelufa Sarwar)').

findings.description: The title 'AGENDA' is centered in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML. The subtitle lines are also centered in PDF but left-aligned in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'AGENDA' centered at top, 'NEUSE RIVER BASIN HYDROLOGIC MODEL' and 'TECHNICAL REVIEW COMMITTEE' centered. HTML: all left-aligned with a horizontal rule under 'AGENDA'.

findings.description: The address and date/time information is centered in the PDF but left-aligned in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF: '512 N. Salisbury Street', venue, city, time, and date all centered. HTML: all left-aligned.

findings.description: Presenter names appear in parentheses inline with topic text in HTML, whereas in the PDF they are right-aligned in a separate column.
findings.evidence: PDF: '(Neelufa Sarwar)' right-aligned. HTML: '(Neelufa Sarwar)' appended to the topic text.

findings.description: The document uses only h1 for 'AGENDA' but does not use any subheadings (h2, h3, etc.) for the agenda items. While the PDF uses a visual layout with horizontal rules separating items, the HTML renders all agenda items as plain paragraphs with no heading hierarchy. This is a structural choice rather than an error, but it does not reflect the document's implicit structure well.
findings.evidence:
Agenda items like 'Review Agenda and Brief Introductions', 'Project Finance Update', 'Project Status', etc. are all rendered as

tags rather than any heading level.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements, with the document content appearing inside an inner <main> tag within the outer <main> tag from the template. This is invalid HTML structure but does not cause content duplication.
findings.evidence: The template wraps everything in <main>...</main>, and the converted content also uses <main>...</main> inside it, resulting in nested main elements.
